rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed line 13 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) as the unsafe copy, explained argv data can exceed the 256-byte buffer, and identified it as a stack buffer overflow. They explicitly tied attacker-controlled input length to the overflow.
rationale.local_fix: They replaced strcpy with strncpy/snprintf and ensured null termination, but did not include rejecting overlong input before copying. The criterion requires an explicit length check that rejects inputs exceeding the buffer.
rationale.global_fix: They proposed eliminating the fixed-size buffer via malloc sized to the input and suggested safer/bounded string handling (e.g., strlcpy/std::string), which removes raw unbounded string handling from the data path. This satisfies the root-cause remediation requirement.
